name: Bobby Bland
abstract: Robert Calvin Bland (born Robert Calvin Brooks; January 27, 1930 – June 23, 2013), known professionally as Bobby "Blue" Bland, was an American blues singer. Bland developed a sound that mixed gospel with the blues and R&B.

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Poston
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee narrates a story where Jay McShann is mistaken for Bobby Bland by a fan; the interviewee (Ken Poston) has no personal interaction with Bobby Bland.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Poston interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Lloyd
about_quotes.evidence: That’s when I played with those guys, like Howlin’ Wolf and Bobby “Blue” Bland and Roscoe Gordon and stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Lloyd — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Magnusson
about_quotes.evidence: And they were playing Bobby Blue Bland and Ike and Tina Turner and James Brown music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Magnusson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Bobby Blue Bland. We was all down South at the same time... we run into one another... They were doin' the same thing... We used to all meet up together in this restaurant and have a ball.

about_quotes.speaker: James Hunter
about_quotes.evidence: I can’t remember what we would have done now. © Fillius Jazz Archive -5- Actually I think I did things that I thought nobody had covered like “Further On Up the Road” by Bobby Bland.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Hunter interviewed by Monk Rowe, Boston, Massachusetts, July 25, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Ruthie Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I did, I wrote “Singing the Blues” after I went to see Bobby “Blue” Bland play... I got that idea from listening to Bobby “Blue” Bland and his horns
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruthie Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 28, 2023
